message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix J-005 in tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
What: T3 transient tests now assert the exact transient class (not the base ReasoningError that RateLimit/Permanent subclass) so a mis-classification is caught.
Test(s): test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient, test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient, test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient (_assert_transient)
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	_HANG = object()  # sentinel: query awaits "forever" to simulate a hung subprocess (#127)
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
63	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream.
64	
65	    Exposes the captured ClaudeAgentOptions via ``scripted.captured_options`` so tests can assert
66	    the security wiring (env=scrubbed_env, sandbox flags) actually reaches the subprocess.
67	    """
68	    import anyio
69	
70	    class _Scripted(list):
71	        captured_options = None  # query records the ClaudeAgentOptions it received here
72	
73	    scripted = _Scripted()
74	
75	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
76	        scripted.captured_options = options
77	        for msg in scripted:
78	            if msg is _HANG:  # simulate a hung subprocess; inner fail_after must cancel us
79	                await anyio.sleep(3600)
80	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
81	                raise msg
82	            yield msg
83	
84	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
85	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
86	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
87	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
88	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
89	    mod.query = query
90	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
91	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
92	    return scripted
93	
94	
95	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
96	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
97	
98	
99	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
100	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
101	    assert _run() == "pong"
102	
103	
104	def test_t3_wires_scrubbed_env_into_subprocess_options(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
105	    # #124: the no-metered-API firewall must be wired end-to-end — env=scrubbed_env() must reach
106	    # the ClaudeAgentOptions handed to the subprocess (not just be unit-tested in isolation).
107	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "[REDACTED]")
108	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://attacker.example")
109	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
110	    _run()
111	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
112	    assert hasattr(opts, "env"), "env not wired into ClaudeAgentOptions (#124 firewall unguarded)"
113	    # the merged child env must neutralize the metered key + off-box redirect, keep OAuth
114	    child = {**__import__("os").environ, **opts.env}
115	    assert child["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] == "" and child["ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL"] == ""
116	    assert child["CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"] == "test-subscription-token"
117	
118	
119	def test_t3_wires_subprocess_sandbox_settings(fake_sdk):
120	    # #125: the subprocess sandbox flags must actually reach the options (no tools, no settings
121	    # sources, non-interactive permission mode).
122	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
123	    _run()
124	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
125	    assert opts.allowed_tools == []  # no tools may run in a reasoning call
126	    assert opts.setting_sources == []  # do not load ambient project/user settings
127	    assert opts.permission_mode == "dontAsk"  # non-interactive, deny-by-default
128	
129	
130	def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
131	    # #127: a hung stream must be cancelled by the inner timeout (frees the pool slot) and
132	    # surface a typed ReasoningError — not block forever.
133	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", 0.3)
134	    fake_sdk.append(_HANG)
135	    import time as _t
136	
137	    t0 = _t.monotonic()
138	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
139	        _run()
140	    assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
141	
142	
143	def _assert_transient(excinfo):
144	    # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
145	    # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
146	    # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
147	    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
148	        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
149	    )
150	
151	
152	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
153	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
154	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
155	        _run()
156	    _assert_transient(ei)
157	
158	
159	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
160	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
161	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
162	        _run()
163	
164	
165	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
166	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
167	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
168	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
169	    assert _run() == "ok"
170	
171	
172	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
173	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
174	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
175	        _run()
176	
177	
178	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
179	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
180	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
181	        _run()
182	    _assert_transient(ei)  # a 500 must be transient, NOT permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
183	
184	
185	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
186	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
187	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
188	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
189	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
190	        _run()
191	
192	
193	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
194	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
195	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
196	        _run()
197	
198	
199	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
200	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
201	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
202	        _run()
203	
204	
205	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
206	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
207	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
208	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
209	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
210	        _run()
211	
212	
213	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
214	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
215	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
216	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
217	        _run()
218	    _assert_transient(ei)  # transport raise must be transient, not permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
219	
220	
221	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
222	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
223	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
224	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
225	        _run()
226

content.text: Let me look at the exact classification block (lines 480-502) to confirm the transient paths raise base `ReasoningError`.

content.content:
66	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
67	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
68	
69	
70	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
71	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
72	
73	
74	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
75	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
76	
77

content.text: Hierarchy confirmed: both `PermanentReasoningError` and `RateLimitError` subclass `ReasoningError`. Now let me verify the three transient paths actually raise the base class (so the discriminator passes) and confirm the test discriminates by checking the classification logic.

content.text: I have all I need to confirm. Let me check one subtlety: line 488-489 catches the SDK-raise (RuntimeError) and re-raises base `ReasoningError`. But `_drain` calls `_drain_stream` which may itself raise `RateLimitError`/`PermanentReasoningError`? Let me check whether the rate-limit/permanent classification happens inside the drain (which would get clobbered by line 489's catch-all).

content.content:
447	    async def _drain() -> None:
448	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
449	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
450	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
451	        #
452	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
453	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
454	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
455	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
456	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
457	        nonlocal result_error
458	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
459	            await _drain_stream()
460	
461	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
462	        nonlocal result_error
463	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
464	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
465	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
466	                if rl == "rejected":
467	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
468	                continue
469	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
470	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
471	                continue
472	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
473	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
474	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):

476	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):

479	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429

483	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
484	                    }

content.text:
The classification logic confirms everything. Let me verify the three named tests discriminate by checking what each path produces:

**Mapping analysis:**

1. `test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient` — appends `_AssistantMessage([])` (no text blocks). Drain completes with no error, `text` is empty → line 502 raises base `ReasoningError`. `_assert_transient` passes (not RateLimit/Permanent). If someone mis-changed line 502 to raise `RateLimitError` or `PermanentReasoningError`, the old `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` would still pass but the new `_assert_transient` fails. Discriminates.

2. `test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient` — `_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500)`. status=500 is not in (429,529) nor (401,403) → line 498 raises base `ReasoningError`. If misclassified as permanent (e.g., 500 fell into a permanent bucket), `_assert_transient` catches it. Discriminates.

3. `test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient` — `RuntimeError` raised inside the stream → caught at line 488, re-raised as base `ReasoningError` at 489. If the catch were narrowed/reclassified, `_assert_transient` catches it. Discriminates.

**Discrimination proof:** Each transient path raises the base `ReasoningError`. `RateLimitError` and `PermanentReasoningError` are subclasses. The old assertion `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` would pass for ALL THREE classes — so it could not detect a mis-classification toward rate-limit/permanent. The new `_assert_transient` asserts `not isinstance(value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError))`, which fails specifically if the transient mapping is reverted to a decisive subclass. This is exactly the J-005 gap.

Let me confirm the tests actually run and pass (current code is correct, so they should pass).

excinfo = <ExceptionInfo PermanentReasoningError('high-stakes tier call failed: connection reset') tblen=3>

    def _assert_transient(excinfo):
        # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
        # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
        # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
>       assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
            f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
        )

input.reasoning:
APPROPRIATE: The fix is correct, idiomatic, and regression-free. The error hierarchy in src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py confirms the premise: PermanentReasoningError (line 70) and RateLimitError (line 74) both subclass ReasoningError (line 66). Therefore pytest.raises(ReasoningError) is satisfied by ALL THREE classes and cannot detect a mis-classification toward a decisive subclass. The new _assert_transient helper (lines 143-149) asserts `not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError))`, which precisely closes that gap. The three transient code paths it guards genuinely raise the base ReasoningError: empty reply -> line 502; generic non-(429/529/401/403) status e.g. 500 -> line 498; SDK raises mid-stream -> caught and re-raised at line 489. No production code changed; test-only edit; no regression. The helper is reused across all three tests (DRY) and well-documented.

COMPLETE (DISCRIMINATES): Empirically proven. With current code all three named tests pass (.venv/bin/python -m pytest -> 3 passed). I then reverted the three transient mappings to decisive subclasses (line 502 ReasoningError->RateLimitError, line 498 ->PermanentReasoningError, line 489 ->PermanentReasoningError) and re-ran: all three tests FAILED on the _assert_transient assertion (e.g. "expected a transient ReasoningError, got PermanentReasoningError"). The old assertion would have stayed green under that revert; the new one fails. seam.py restored, git diff clean. The tests fail-on-revert exactly as required.

content.text: CONFIRMED. The J-005 fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py is both appropriate and complete. The new `_assert_transient` helper closes a real discrimination gap (RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both subclass ReasoningError, so the prior `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` could not catch a transient-to-decisive mis-classification), and I empirically proved all three named tests fail when the transient mappings are reverted and pass with the correct code.
